title: Amdahl's Law — Parallel Speedup
content: The maximum theoretical speedup of a program using P processors is S = 1 / ((1 − p) + p/P), where p is the parallelisable fraction.
extended_content: As P → ∞, speedup converges to 1/(1−p).  A program that is 90% parallel can never exceed 10× speedup regardless of the number of processors.  Gustafson's Law provides a more optimistic view when problem size scales with processor count.
source: Amdahl, G. (1967). Validity of the single processor approach.
